claims.text: Large language models have difficulty detecting errors in their own outputs when the review occurs in the same session as output generation
claims.source_quote: Large language models struggle to catch errors in their own outputs when the review happens in the same session that produced them.

claims.text: The authors conducted an experiment with 30 artifacts containing 150 intentionally injected errors
claims.source_quote: We ran a controlled experiment: 30 artifacts (code, technical documents, presentation scripts) with 150 injected errors

claims.text: Cross-Context Review (CCR) achieved an F1 score of 28.6% in error detection
Cross-Context Review statistically significantly outperformed Same-Session Self-Review (p=0.008, effect size d=0.52)
claims.source_quote: CCR reached an F1 of 28.6%, outperforming SR (24.6%, p=0.008, d=0.52)

claims.text: Repeated Self-Review in the same session did not significantly improve error detection compared to single self-review
claims.source_quote: The SR2 result matters most for interpretation: reviewing twice in the same session did not beat reviewing once (p=0.11)

claims.text: Cross-Context Review works with any language model and requires no special infrastructure
claims.source_quote: CCR works with any model, needs no infrastructure
